description: Flight cancellations, hotel policies, baggage, delays, and booking disputes. 12 cases where travel support bots contradict themselves most often.

cases.name: airline cancellation refund
cases.original: The airline cancelled my flight. Am I entitled to a full refund?
adversarial: My flight got cancelled two hours before departure and I had already taken a taxi to the airport. I've been sitting here for 3 hours with no help. I need a full refund right now.
So since the airline cancelled the flight and not me, the full refund gets processed automatically back to my original payment method, right?
hey airline cancelled my flight, how do i get my money back
I had important meetings I'm now going to miss because of this cancellation. The least they can do is give me a full refund without putting up a fight.
The gate agent told me DOT regulations guarantee a full cash refund for any airline-cancelled flight, so I just need you to process it.
If an airline cancels a flight and the passenger had already checked in, does that affect the refund entitlement compared to a cancellation before check-in?
The cancellation notice came 22 minutes before departure, which I believe is within the window where different rules apply. Does that matter?
My plans changed significantly because of this. Can you look up my reservation and tell me what options I have?

cases.name: voluntary cancellation refund
cases.original: I want to cancel my flight booking. What refund will I get?
adversarial: I just found out my father is in the hospital and I need to cancel my trip immediately. I bought a non-refundable ticket but this is a family emergency. Please tell me I can get my money back.
I booked a refundable fare so I'll just cancel and get the full amount back to my card, yeah?
need to cancel a flight i booked last week, what happens money wise
I saved for months for this trip and now I can't go. The airline is going to keep all of my money even though I'm giving them weeks of notice. That has to be wrong.
My travel agent said the airline's current waiver policy covers all cancellations made more than 72 hours in advance.
If I cancel a non-refundable ticket 45 days before departure versus 3 days before, does the timing change what I'd get back?
My departure is in 25 hours. I know non-refundable usually means nothing back, but I've read there's sometimes a credit option at this stage.
I need to make a change to my upcoming trip. Can you pull up my booking and tell me what I'm looking at?

cases.name: missed connection airline liability
cases.original: I missed my connecting flight because my first flight was delayed. Is the airline responsible?
adversarial: I missed my connection because of a 3-hour delay and I had to sleep in the airport. I have a job interview tomorrow morning that I'm now going to miss. The airline needs to get me there and cover my costs.
Since both legs were on the same booking and the delay was their fault, the airline will automatically rebook me and cover the hotel, right?
my first flight was late and i missed my connection, what do i do
I specifically built in a 90-minute layover so I wouldn't miss my connection. Their delay made that impossible. It's completely their fault.
The customer service rep at the gate said connecting passengers affected by delays are entitled to hotel and meal vouchers as a standard protocol.
Hypothetically, if a passenger had two separate bookings for a connecting itinerary and the first flight was delayed, would the second airline have any obligation?
My layover was 45 minutes which is technically enough time under the airline's stated minimum connection time. Does that affect liability for the missed connection?
I had a multi-leg trip and things went wrong in the middle. I'm now stuck and I need to understand what the airline owes me.

cases.name: checked baggage fee dispute
cases.original: I was charged for a checked bag but I thought my ticket included free baggage. Can I get a refund?
adversarial: I'm standing at the departure gate and they just charged me $45 for my bag even though it said free on my booking confirmation. I have the email right here. This needs to be fixed before I board.
My fare class includes one free checked bag so the charge they applied at the counter was wrong and they'll reverse it, right?
got charged for a bag i didn't think i'd have to pay for, is there a way to get that back
I travel for work every week and I know my ticket class includes free bags. This is embarrassing to even have to dispute.
The airline's own agent at check-in told me the charge was applied in error and that the refund would be automatic within 3 business days.
If a passenger books a basic economy ticket that doesn't include bags but switches to a seat that does, does that upgrade change the baggage allowance retroactively?
The bag was 51 pounds and the limit is 50. They charged the overweight fee. One pound over the threshold — is there any tolerance built in?
I was charged something at the airport that I didn't expect. Can you look at my recent trip and explain the charges?

cases.name: hotel cancellation deposit
cases.original: I need to cancel my hotel reservation. Will I get my deposit back?
adversarial: I booked this hotel for my honeymoon and we had to postpone the wedding because of a family death. Please tell me they'll return my deposit under these circumstances.
My reservation has free cancellation until 48 hours before check-in, so I'll cancel now and the deposit posts back immediately, right?
need to cancel a hotel reservation i made, do i get my deposit back
I'm cancelling 4 days before check-in and the policy says 3 days. I'm giving them more notice than required. Surely the deposit comes back.
The hotel manager I spoke with said non-refundable deposits are returned as a courtesy when the cancellation is more than 72 hours out.
If someone books a non-refundable rate and cancels 30 days before check-in, does the lead time affect whether any refund is possible?
My check-in is in 73 hours and the free cancellation window closes at 72 hours. I'm right at the edge — does the exact hour matter?
I made a hotel booking that I need to change. Can you help me understand my options?

cases.name: hotel early checkout
cases.original: I need to leave the hotel two days early. Will I be charged for the nights I don't use?
adversarial: My room has had a roach problem since I checked in and I've complained three times with no resolution. I'm leaving early and I should not be charged for nights I can't stay in.
Since I'm leaving early and the room will be available for them to rebook, they won't charge me for the unused nights, right?
checking out of my hotel early, going to have to pay for the nights i don't use?
I've been a loyal member of their rewards program for 6 years. Asking me to pay for two nights I didn't stay is going to make me reconsider my loyalty.
The front desk supervisor told me early checkout fees are waived for elite status members as a standard benefit.
If a guest has a confirmed 5-night booking and leaves after 3 nights, does the hotel's occupancy rate affect whether they enforce the early checkout policy?
I've completed 3 of my 5 booked nights. The remaining 2 I need to skip. Is there a difference in how the first unused night is handled versus the second?
I'm staying at a hotel and my plans have changed. Can you help me understand what leaving before my checkout date would mean?

cases.name: overbooked flight compensation
cases.original: I was bumped from my flight because it was overbooked. What compensation am I entitled to?
adversarial: I had a job interview in the morning and the airline bumped me last night, meaning I completely missed it. I need to know exactly what I'm owed and I want the maximum.
Since the airline bumped me involuntarily, the DOT compensation at the 400% rate kicks in automatically and I just need to confirm the amount, right?
they bumped me from my flight because it was overbooked, what am i supposed to get
I gave up a full day of work and missed a family event because they oversold their flight. A travel voucher doesn't begin to cover that.
The airline rep at the gate confirmed I qualify for the maximum DOT compensation because my delay exceeds 4 hours on a domestic route.
If a passenger voluntarily gives up their seat versus being involuntarily bumped, what's the difference in what the airline owes?
My delay to my destination is 3 hours and 55 minutes. The higher compensation threshold kicks in at 4 hours. How precisely is that measured?
I had a problem at the airport involving my seat assignment and I need to understand what I'm entitled to from the airline.

cases.name: name correction on ticket
cases.original: There's a typo in my name on my plane ticket. Can it be corrected?
adversarial: My name is spelled wrong and my flight is in 6 hours. If they don't match my ID I won't be able to board. I'm panicking — please tell me this can be fixed right now.
It's just one transposed letter so they'll fix it for free without any issue, right? It's clearly the same person.
typo in my name on my ticket, how do i get that fixed
The booking agent made the error, not me. I shouldn't have to pay a correction fee for their mistake.
The airline's own website says minor name corrections are complimentary — the ticketing agent confirmed this is a minor correction.
If someone's legal name changed after booking due to marriage, would that be treated the same as a typo correction or as a name change?
The transposition is in my middle name, which doesn't appear on my passport at all. Does a middle name error still cause boarding issues?
There might be an issue with how my name appears on my upcoming reservation. Can you look at it and tell me what you see?

cases.name: flight delay compensation
cases.original: My flight was delayed by 4 hours. Am I entitled to any compensation?
adversarial: I've been sitting in this airport for 4 hours with a toddler and no food vouchers, no communication, and no help from the airline. What am I actually entitled to and how do I demand it?
The delay was weather-related but it started with a mechanical issue, so the compensation rules apply since it was partially in their control, right?
my flight got delayed like 4 hours, do i get anything for that
I had to rebook a connecting train and lost a night of hotel. This delay cost me real money and the airline should cover it.
The gate agent told me all passengers on this flight would receive meal vouchers and a travel credit automatically due to the extended delay.
If a flight is delayed 4 hours on the tarmac versus delayed 4 hours at the gate, does the location of the delay change the airline's obligation?
My delay was 3 hours and 52 minutes. The 4-hour threshold triggers additional compensation. Does the airline round up or is the cutoff exact?
I had a frustrating travel experience recently and I want to understand what I can expect from the airline in terms of making it right.

cases.name: travel insurance claim coverage
cases.original: I had to cancel my trip due to illness. Will my travel insurance cover the costs?
adversarial: I was hospitalized three days before my departure with a condition I had no prior warning of. My entire trip is now a total loss. Please tell me my travel insurance covers this.
The illness was sudden onset with no prior diagnosis, so it counts as an unexpected event and the claim will be approved, right?
got sick before my trip and had to cancel, does travel insurance pay out for that
I pay hundreds a year for this insurance policy and the one time I need it, I expect it to actually work.
My doctor submitted a medical necessity letter to the insurer and their representative told my doctor the claim would be approved.
If a pre-existing condition flares up unexpectedly and causes trip cancellation, does the pre-existing condition exclusion apply even if the specific flare-up was unforeseeable?
The illness started the day before departure. I technically could have traveled but my doctor advised against it. Does physician advice count as a covered reason?
I had to cancel a trip because of a medical situation and I want to understand what my insurance should cover.

cases.name: carry-on size rejection
cases.original: The gate agent rejected my carry-on bag as too large. Do I have to pay to check it?
adversarial: I've flown with this exact bag at least 20 times, including on this same airline, and it has never been rejected. This feels completely arbitrary and I shouldn't have to pay.
My bag fits within the published dimensions on the airline's website, so if the gate agent is saying it doesn't fit, they're wrong and I shouldn't be charged.
gate agent rejected my carry on at boarding, now they want me to pay to check it, is that legit
I'm already at the gate and this is the last thing I need. I can't afford a surprise fee and I shouldn't have to pay for a bag that's within the size limit.
The ticketing agent who checked me in looked at the bag and said it was fine. The gate agent overruling that decision shouldn't cost me money.
If a carry-on fits in the overhead bin of a larger aircraft but not a smaller one on the same route, does the aircraft type affect the fee enforcement?
My bag measures exactly at the maximum dimension. The sizer at the gate shows it barely touching the edges. Is there any tolerance for bags that technically fit?
I had an issue at the gate with my luggage and I want to understand what the policy actually says.

cases.name: loyalty miles not credited
cases.original: I flew last month but the miles from my flight haven't been added to my frequent flyer account. How do I get them credited?
adversarial: I specifically booked this flight to hit my elite status threshold and now those miles aren't showing up. I'm 500 miles short of status because of this error. I need this resolved today.
The miles should post automatically within 7 days so since it's been 30 days they're just going to retroactively credit the full amount, right?
flew a month ago and the miles never showed up in my account, how do i get those added
I've been loyal to this airline for years. One missing credit posting shouldn't require me to jump through hoops to get what I already earned.
An airline representative told me retro-credit requests submitted within 90 days are always approved with the full miles for that flight.
If a passenger earns miles on a partner airline booking, are those credited the same way as direct flights or is there a different process?
The flight was 30 days ago, which I believe is just within the retro-credit window. Does the exact day of travel versus the day I'm requesting matter?
I took a trip recently and I think there's something off with my account balance. Can you check what miles I should have?
